intro: Torularia tetracnodides, commonly known as Indian turnip or white turnip, is a perennial plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its white flowers and edible roots. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Torularia tetracnodides include isothiocyanates, glucosinolates, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
